summary: bin/rubocop is clean (145 files, no offenses) and the Minitest suite passes in full when run serially (184 runs, 660 assertions, 0 failures, 0 errors on a clean rerun; an earlier run showed transient PreparedStatementCacheExpired errors that did not reproduce and are environment flakiness, not a code defect). However, inspecting the compiled Tailwind output (app/assets/builds/tailwind.css) surfaced a real, severe bug in the new dark-mode CSS: a code comment in app/assets/tailwind/application.css contains a literal `*/` inside the phrase 'raw gray-*/white', which prematurely closes the comment. The remainder of the intended comment text becomes invalid stray CSS, and this swallows the entire light-mode `:root { ... }` block (lines 11-21) that defines the default values for --color-app, --color-surface, --color-surface-hover, --color-border, --color-text, --color-text-muted, --color-text-subtle, --color-nav-active-bg, and --color-nav-active-text. Confirmed by grepping the compiled build: only the `.dark { ... }` override block for these custom properties is present; the light-mode `:root` definitions are entirely absent from the output. Since dozens of views were swept to use the semantic bg-app/bg-surface/text-default/border-default/etc. utilities that read these custom properties, every page renders with undefined (effectively unset) colors whenever the `dark` class is not applied -- i.e. by default for any user whose system preference is light, or after an explicit toggle to light mode. This is not caught by ruby -c, rubocop, or the Minitest suite (none of which parse/render compiled CSS), so it slipped through the implementer's stated verification. Test suite and lint are otherwise solid; this one CSS defect needs a fix (e.g. rewording the comment to avoid an embedded `*/`, such as 'raw gray-* / white') before this can ship.

findings.issue: The comment spanning lines 7-10 contains a literal `*/` inside the words 'raw gray-*/white', which closes the CSS comment early. The rest of the intended comment text is left as invalid CSS, and this consumes the light-mode `:root { ... }` block (lines 11-21) that declares the default (non-dark) values for --color-app, --color-surface, --color-surface-hover, --color-border, --color-text, --color-text-muted, --color-text-subtle, --color-nav-active-bg, and --color-nav-active-text. Verified against the compiled app/assets/builds/tailwind.css: only the `.dark { ... }` override for these custom properties exists; the light-mode `:root` declarations are missing entirely. Every view using bg-app, bg-surface, bg-surface-hover, border-default, divide-default, ring-default, text-default, text-muted, text-subtle, bg-nav-active, or text-nav-active (dozens of swept views) will render with these colors undefined whenever the `dark` class is absent -- i.e. the app is broken by default for light-mode/system-light users, which is the majority default path per this feature's own requirement to follow system preference.
